name: golang-github-go-git-go-billy-dev
title: golang-github-go-git-go-billy-dev
summary: missing interface filesystem abstraction for Go
description:
missing interface filesystem abstraction for Go
The missing interface filesystem abstraction for Go.  Billy implements
an interface based on the os standard library, allowing to develop
applications without dependency on the underlying storage. Makes it
virtually free to implement mocks and testing over filesystem operations.
.
Why billy?  The library billy deals with storage systems and Billy
is the name of a well-known, IKEA bookcase. That's it.
use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use golang-github-go-git-go-billy-dev because missing interface filesystem abstraction for Go.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use golang-github-go-git-go-billy-dev as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair golang-github-go-git-go-billy-dev with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `golang-github-go-git-go-billy-dev --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
